manifest.title: RailCross Model Review
manifest.description: Technical audit of the synthetic traffic model.

charts.title: F1 score: traffic-first model versus baseline
charts.description: Held-out synthetic events; higher is better.

query.sql:
SELECT 'Traffic-first model' AS method, 0.9098 AS f1
UNION ALL SELECT 'Baseline rule', 0.504;
query.description: Select the verified held-out F1 values for the trained model and baseline rule.

blocks.body:
## Technical summary

RailCross estimates railway-gate state from 14 traffic-only features: bilateral speeds, stopped ratios, queues, queue growth, delay, jam length, and congestion duration. On 29,900 rows from 650 simulated events, the held-out synthetic test reaches **0.910 F1**, **0.935 precision**, and **0.886 recall** at a validation-selected 0.75 threshold. A separate regressor estimates reopening time with **1.77-minute MAE**. The chart compares the trained model with a 0.504-F1 fixed traffic rule. These are simulator benchmarks, not real-world accuracy.

## Review verdict: share with caveats

The pipeline is reproducible and suitable for a resume prototype only when every metric is labelled **synthetic**. It has not been tested on live vehicle traces or verified gate states. Main risks: simulator-to-model circularity, incomplete crossing coverage, ordinary congestion that may be too easy, and uncalibrated real-world probabilities. The full methodology, feature definitions, validation checks, and next-step plan are in `PROJECT_TECHNICAL_REVIEW.md`.
